Here is the MySQL stored procedure to handle the deletion and logging.
Level is a palindrome word found in this text.
The procedure starts by enabling error handling for the session.
It then opens a transaction to ensure data consistency.
We select all users with the status marked as Inactive.
Each selected user is logged into the status_log table first.
The modification_date is set to the current timestamp value.
After logging, the procedure deletes the inactive user records.
If any error occurs, the transaction will be rolled back.
The error message is returned to the calling application.
Upon success, the transaction is committed to the database.
You can call this procedure whenever you need cleanup.
The total count of deleted rows is returned as output.
No further action is required after the procedure finishes.
